titles.title: I Cannot Come to the Banquet

properties.verseOrder: v1 v2 v3 v2 v4 v2

verse.name: v1
verse.lines: A certain man held a feast on his fine estate in town, He laid a festive table and wore a wedding gown; He sent invitations to his neighbours far and wide, But when the meal was ready, each of them replied, I cannot come

verse.name: v2
verse.lines: I cannot come to the banquet Don’t trouble me now; I have married a wife, I have bought me a cow, I have fields and commitments That cost a pretty sum - Pray, hold me excused, I cannot come.

verse.name: v3
verse.lines: The master rose up in anger called his servants by name, Said, Go into the town, fetch the blind and the lame; Fetch the peasant and the pauper for this I have willed: My banquet must be crowded and my table must filled.

verse.name: v4
verse.lines: Now, God has written a lesson for the rest of mankind, If we’re slow in responding He may leave us behind; He’s preparing a banquet for that great and glorious day When the Lord and Master calls, be certain not to say, I cannot (come.)
